ogTitle: Weddell vs White Gums: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Weddell and White Gums.
heading: Weddell vs White Gums — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Weddell, Northern Territory and White Gums, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Weddell has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to White Gums (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Weddell or White Gums safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Weddell has a flood risk score of 27/100, while White Gums scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Weddell scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. White Gums scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Weddell or White Gums?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Weddell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
